methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Limelight Steel is a pre-seed-stage climate and energy company that has raised $6M to date. The broader Climate & Energy sector comprises 1 public comparable (LNZA) and 8 private peers including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape, though none represent sufficiently tight stage or valuation matches for reliable peer-median benchmarking.

The stage-progression method is the applicable triangulation approach here. Using the $6M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from pre-seed to the subsequent financing stage yields an implied valuation of $15M. This method directly reflects the company's capital trajectory and market expectations for early-stage climate technology companies progressing through funding rounds.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $13M to $17M, centered on $15M. The relatively tight spread reflects reliance on a single method rather than convergence across multiple approaches. Key uncertainties include the absence of directly comparable pre-seed peers in the Climate & Energy space—the peer universe exists but lacks granular stage alignment—and the assumption that standard 2.5x step-up multiples apply uniformly to Limelight's specific technology and market position. Further valuation refinement would benefit from either materialized metrics (revenue, unit economics, pilot progress) or identification of tighter peer comparables at similar funding stages.
